'東京都三宅村伊ヶ谷', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '静岡県熱海市伊豆山', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Sengen-jinja (Mishima)}} [[JA:浅間神社 (三島市)]] {{wikidata link|Q30925511}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name=Asama Shrine |Image=[[File:Sengen Shrine (Mishima) and Shibaoka Shrine.jpg|250px]]
Shibaoka Shrine (left) and Asama Shrine (right)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|7|23|N|138|54|49|E}}}} |Location=6-3 Shibamoto-chō, Mishima City, Shizuoka Prefecture | Latitude degrees = 35 | Latitude minutes = 07 | Latitude seconds = 23.0 | N (North latitude) or S (South latitude) = N | Longitude degrees = 138 | Longitude minutes = 54 | Longitude seconds = 49.0 | E (East longitude) or W (West longitude) = E | Map country code = JP-22 | Map = Japan Shizuoka | Label position=bottom |Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto}}
Hafuhime no Mikoto |Founded= |Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} debated shrine (minor)
Later {{ill|lt=Izumi Province|Q860928|WD=Q860928}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Sannomiya}}, then {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya#Ninomiya, Sannomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Ninomiya}}{{Sfn|Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System|2000|p=162}} |Main hall= |Annual festival={{ill|lt=July 15|Q2686|WD=Q2686}}・{{ill|lt=July 16|Q2731|WD=Q2731|lt=16th}}Explanatory boards at various locations (within the Asama Shrine precincts). }} '''Asama Shrine''' (Sengen Jinja) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Shibamoto-chō, {{ill|lt=Mishima City|Q653478|WD=Q653478}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}. It was formerly the Sannomiya, later the Ninomiya, of {{ill|lt=Izumi Province|Q860928|WD=Q860928}}. It is one of the {{ill|lt=Asama Shrine|Q3471785|WD=Q3471785}} shrines. === Overview === In ancient times it was one of the eight auxiliary shrines (Hassho Bekū) of {{ill|lt=Mishima Taisha|Q650772|WD=Q650772}}{{Sfn|History of Mishima City, Vol. 2|1988|p=558}}. It was also regarded as a {{ill|lt=Sessha|Q135009904|WD=Q135009904}} (subsidiary shrine), but is now independent. Although it is listed as a candidate shrine for the Shikinaisha “Iga Muhime no Mikoto Shrine,” it is not considered a leading theory. Generally, this Himusubi no Mikoto Shrine is thought to be the Raiden Shrine, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|lt=Izusan Shrine|Q611460|WD=Q611460}} in {{ill|lt=Atami City|Q653364|WD=Q653364}}, and there is a theory that this shrine served as its distant worship site{{Sfn|Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System|2000|p=162}}. The shrine name is said to have shifted from the Buddhist name “Sengen” of the honji-butsu (original Buddhist manifestation) of Izusan Shrine—{{ill|lt=Senju Kannon|Q10904910|WD=Q10904910|lt=Eleven-Faced Thousand-Armed Thousand-Eyed Kannon}}—to the kami name “Asama”{{Sfn|Revised Tōshū Shikō, Vol. 8 (Upper)|p=35}}. According to the “{{ill|lt=Izumi Province Shrine Rank Register|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087}},” its divine rank is “Senior First Rank, Thousand-Eyed Great Bodhisattva.” It was originally the Sannomiya of Izumi Province, but after the Ninomiya Hachimangū (present-day Wakamiya Shrine) was transferred to the grounds of the grand shrine, it became the Ninomiya{{Sfn|Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System|2000|p=162}}. However, there is no record that the Shinnomiya, Hirose Shrine, was elevated to Sannomiya{{Sfn|Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System|2000|p=162}}, and one theory holds that the Sannomiya was Yōhara Shrine{{Sfn|History of Mishima City, Vol. 2|1988|p=559}}. The annual festival is held on July 15 and 16. It is said that in the past, people always visited this shrine when making a {{ill|lt=Mount Fuji pilgrimage|Q11456115|WD=Q11456115}}. In addition, the spring water around the precincts served as irrigation water for downstream towns and villages. === Enshrined deities === [[File:Sengen-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|thumb|Asama Shrine worship hall]] * Principal deities ** {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto}} ** Hafuhime no Mikoto * Associated deities ** {{ill|lt=Ninigi|Q1056017|WD=Q1056017|lt=Ninigi no Mikoto}} ** {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|lt=Hoakari no Mikoto}} ** {{ill|lt=Hohoderi|Q10731395|WD=Q10731395|lt=Hoderi no Mikoto}} ** {{ill|lt=Hoori|Q1051960|WD=Q1051960|lt=Hoori no Mikoto}} Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto is the daughter of the Mishima Ōkami ({{ill|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi no Mikoto}}), the enshrined deity of Mishima Taisha. According to the “{{ill|lt=Miyake-ki|Q17215100|WD=Q17215100}},” Hafuhime no Mikoto was a consort whom the Mishima Ōkami placed on {{ill|lt=Izu Ōshima|Q1070580|WD=Q1070580|lt=Ōshima}}. Furthermore, the associated deity Ninigi no Mikoto is the husband of Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto, and Hoakari no Mikoto, Hoderi no Mikoto, and Hoori no Mikoto are their sons. In other words, the shrine enshrines the wife and daughter of the deity of Mishima Taisha, as well as the daughter’s family. === Shrine within the precincts === [[File:Shibaoka Shrine.jpg|thumb|Shibaoka Shrine]] ; Shibaoka Shrine :* Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Takami-Musubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi no Kami}} :* Shrine rank: {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system#Minor shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Village shrine}} :* Annual festival: July 15–16 : It is enshrined to the left of the main hall of Asama Shrine. Originally, it was a residence shrine enshrined on the estate of the Seko Rokudayū family of the Seko honjin, but in {{ill|lt=Meiji 4|Q11512280|WD=Q11512280}} ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746}} or {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}}) it came to be worshiped as the tutelary shrine of Shiba Town and was ranked as a village shrine. In March {{ill|lt=1877|Q7773|WD=Q7773}} (Meiji 10), it was destroyed in the great fire of {{ill|lt=Mishima-juku|Q524171|WD=Q524171}}, but was restored in {{ill|lt=1880|Q7806|WD=Q7806}} (Meiji 13). In July 1952 it was merged into Asama Shrine and relocated to the present site together with its shrine building. Beneath the main hall is a large circular stone called the “spirit stone,” which is regarded as a guardian deity for children. === Sources === [[File:Sengen Shrine (Mishima).jpg|thumb|Asama Shrine torii]] === References and websites === * Explanatory boards within the precincts '''Historical materials''' * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=Compiled by Akira Akiyama; revised by Shōhei Hagiwara|author=|year=|title=Revised Tōshū Shikō, Vol. 8 (Upper)|publisher=|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Revised Tōshū Shikō, Vol. 8 (Upper)}}}} ** {{PDFlink|[https://archive.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kosho/ru04/ru04_00291/ru04_00291_0010/ru04_00291_0010.pdf “Revised Tōshū Shikō, Vol. 8 (Upper)”]}} (Waseda University Library), consulted. '''Books''' * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=History of Mishima City Editorial Committee|author=|year=1988|title=History of Mishima City, Vol. 2|publisher=Mishima City|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|History of Mishima City, Vol. 2|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System Research Association|author=|year=2000|chapter=|title=Foundational Research on the Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System|publisher=Iwata Shoin|isbn=978-4872941708|ref={{Harvid|Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System|2000}}}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Asama Shrine|Q3471785|WD=Q3471785}} * {{ill|lt=Mishima Taisha|Q650772|WD=Q650772}} * {{ill|lt=Izusan Shrine|Q611460|WD=Q611460}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Izumi Province|Q11380552|WD=Q11380552}} * {{ill|lt=Izumi Province Shrine Rank Register|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087}} === External links === * [http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402034 Asama Shrine] – Shizuoka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines {{DEFAULTSORT:sengenjinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sengen Shrine (Izu Province Ni-no-Miya)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sengen Shrine (Izu Province Ni-no-Miya)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Ni-no-Miya]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Successor]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:三島市の歴史]] [[Category:三島市の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社|小せんけんしんしや]] [[Category:二宮]] [[Category:三宮 (社格)]] [[Category:伊豆国|社2]] [[Category:浅間神社|せんけんしんしや みしまし]]

'東京都三宅村伊ヶ谷', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '静岡県熱海市伊豆山', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Sengen-jinja (Mishima)}} [[JA:浅間神社 (三島市)]] {{wikidata link|Q30925511}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name=Asama Shrine |Image=[[File:Sengen Shrine (Mishima) and Shibaoka Shrine.jpg|250px]]
Shibaoka Shrine (left) and Asama Shrine (right)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|7|23|N|138|54|49|E}}}} |Location=6-3 Shibamoto-chō, Mishima City, Shizuoka Prefecture | Latitude degrees = 35 | Latitude minutes = 07 | Latitude seconds = 23.0 | N (North latitude) or S (South latitude) = N | Longitude degrees = 138 | Longitude minutes = 54 | Longitude seconds = 49.0 | E (East longitude) or W (West longitude) = E | Map country code = JP-22 | Map = Japan Shizuoka | Label position=bottom |Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto}}
Hafuhime no Mikoto |Founded= |Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} debated shrine (minor)
Later {{ill|lt=Izumi Province|Q860928|WD=Q860928}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Sannomiya}}, then {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya#Ninomiya, Sannomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Ninomiya}}{{Sfn|Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System|2000|p=162}} |Main hall= |Annual festival={{ill|lt=July 15|Q2686|WD=Q2686}}・{{ill|lt=July 16|Q2731|WD=Q2731|lt=16th}}Explanatory boards at various locations (within the Asama Shrine precincts). }} '''Asama Shrine''' (Sengen Jinja) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Shibamoto-chō, {{ill|lt=Mishima City|Q653478|WD=Q653478}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}. It was formerly the Sannomiya, later the Ninomiya, of {{ill|lt=Izumi Province|Q860928|WD=Q860928}}. It is one of the {{ill|lt=Asama Shrine|Q3471785|WD=Q3471785}} shrines. === Overview === In ancient times it was one of the eight auxiliary shrines (Hassho Bekū) of {{ill|lt=Mishima Taisha|Q650772|WD=Q650772}}{{Sfn|History of Mishima City, Vol. 2|1988|p=558}}. It was also regarded as a {{ill|lt=Sessha|Q135009904|WD=Q135009904}} (subsidiary shrine), but is now independent. Although it is listed as a candidate shrine for the Shikinaisha “Iga Muhime no Mikoto Shrine,” it is not considered a leading theory. Generally, this Himusubi no Mikoto Shrine is thought to be the Raiden Shrine, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|lt=Izusan Shrine|Q611460|WD=Q611460}} in {{ill|lt=Atami City|Q653364|WD=Q653364}}, and there is a theory that this shrine served as its distant worship site{{Sfn|Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System|2000|p=162}}. The shrine name is said to have shifted from the Buddhist name “Sengen” of the honji-butsu (original Buddhist manifestation) of Izusan Shrine—{{ill|lt=Senju Kannon|Q10904910|WD=Q10904910|lt=Eleven-Faced Thousand-Armed Thousand-Eyed Kannon}}—to the kami name “Asama”{{Sfn|Revised Tōshū Shikō, Vol. 8 (Upper)|p=35}}. According to the “{{ill|lt=Izumi Province Shrine Rank Register|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087}},” its divine rank is “Senior First Rank, Thousand-Eyed Great Bodhisattva.” It was originally the Sannomiya of Izumi Province, but after the Ninomiya Hachimangū (present-day Wakamiya Shrine) was transferred to the grounds of the grand shrine, it became the Ninomiya{{Sfn|Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System|2000|p=162}}. However, there is no record that the Shinnomiya, Hirose Shrine, was elevated to Sannomiya{{Sfn|Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System|2000|p=162}}, and one theory holds that the Sannomiya was Yōhara Shrine{{Sfn|History of Mishima City, Vol. 2|1988|p=559}}. The annual festival is held on July 15 and 16. It is said that in the past, people always visited this shrine when making a {{ill|lt=Mount Fuji pilgrimage|Q11456115|WD=Q11456115}}. In addition, the spring water around the precincts served as irrigation water for downstream towns and villages. === Enshrined deities === [[File:Sengen-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|thumb|Asama Shrine worship hall]] * Principal deities ** {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto}} ** Hafuhime no Mikoto * Associated deities ** {{ill|lt=Ninigi|Q1056017|WD=Q1056017|lt=Ninigi no Mikoto}} ** {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|lt=Hoakari no Mikoto}} ** {{ill|lt=Hohoderi|Q10731395|WD=Q10731395|lt=Hoderi no Mikoto}} ** {{ill|lt=Hoori|Q1051960|WD=Q1051960|lt=Hoori no Mikoto}} Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto is the daughter of the Mishima Ōkami ({{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi no Mikoto}}), the enshrined deity of Mishima Taisha. According to the “{{ill|lt=Miyake-ki|Q17215100|WD=Q17215100}},” Hafuhime no Mikoto was a consort whom the Mishima Ōkami placed on {{ill|lt=Izu Ōshima|Q1070580|WD=Q1070580|lt=Ōshima}}. Furthermore, the associated deity Ninigi no Mikoto is the husband of Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto, and Hoakari no Mikoto, Hoderi no Mikoto, and Hoori no Mikoto are their sons. In other words, the shrine enshrines the wife and daughter of the deity of Mishima Taisha, as well as the daughter’s family. === Shrine within the precincts === [[File:Shibaoka Shrine.jpg|thumb|Shibaoka Shrine]] ; Shibaoka Shrine :* Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Takami-Musubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi no Kami}} :* Shrine rank: {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system#Minor shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Village shrine}} :* Annual festival: July 15–16 : It is enshrined to the left of the main hall of Asama Shrine. Originally, it was a residence shrine enshrined on the estate of the Seko Rokudayū family of the Seko honjin, but in {{ill|lt=Meiji 4|Q11512280|WD=Q11512280}} ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746}} or {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}}) it came to be worshiped as the tutelary shrine of Shiba Town and was ranked as a village shrine. In March {{ill|lt=1877|Q7773|WD=Q7773}} (Meiji 10), it was destroyed in the great fire of {{ill|lt=Mishima-juku|Q524171|WD=Q524171}}, but was restored in {{ill|lt=1880|Q7806|WD=Q7806}} (Meiji 13). In July 1952 it was merged into Asama Shrine and relocated to the present site together with its shrine building. Beneath the main hall is a large circular stone called the “spirit stone,” which is regarded as a guardian deity for children. === Sources === [[File:Sengen Shrine (Mishima).jpg|thumb|Asama Shrine torii]] === References and websites === * Explanatory boards within the precincts '''Historical materials''' * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=Compiled by Akira Akiyama; revised by Shōhei Hagiwara|author=|year=|title=Revised Tōshū Shikō, Vol. 8 (Upper)|publisher=|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Revised Tōshū Shikō, Vol. 8 (Upper)}}}} ** {{PDFlink|[https://archive.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kosho/ru04/ru04_00291/ru04_00291_0010/ru04_00291_0010.pdf “Revised Tōshū Shikō, Vol. 8 (Upper)”]}} (Waseda University Library), consulted. '''Books''' * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=History of Mishima City Editorial Committee|author=|year=1988|title=History of Mishima City, Vol. 2|publisher=Mishima City|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|History of Mishima City, Vol. 2|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System Research Association|author=|year=2000|chapter=|title=Foundational Research on the Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System|publisher=Iwata Shoin|isbn=978-4872941708|ref={{Harvid|Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System|2000}}}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Asama Shrine|Q3471785|WD=Q3471785}} * {{ill|lt=Mishima Taisha|Q650772|WD=Q650772}} * {{ill|lt=Izusan Shrine|Q611460|WD=Q611460}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Izumi Province|Q11380552|WD=Q11380552}} * {{ill|lt=Izumi Province Shrine Rank Register|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087}} === External links === * [http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402034 Asama Shrine] – Shizuoka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines {{DEFAULTSORT:sengenjinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sengen Shrine (Izu Province Ni-no-Miya)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sengen Shrine (Izu Province Ni-no-Miya)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Ni-no-Miya]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Successor]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:三島市の歴史]] [[Category:三島市の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社|小せんけんしんしや]] [[Category:二宮]] [[Category:三宮 (社格)]] [[Category:伊豆国|社2]] [[Category:浅間神社|せんけんしんしや みしまし]]

Hafuhime no Mikoto |Founded= |Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} debated shrine (minor)
Later {{ill|lt=Izumi Province|Q860928|WD=Q860928}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Sannomiya}}, then {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya#Ninomiya, Sannomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Ninomiya}}{{Sfn|Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System|2000|p=162}} |Main hall= |Annual festival={{ill|lt=July 15|Q2686|WD=Q2686}}・{{ill|lt=July 16|Q2731|WD=Q2731|lt=16th}}Explanatory boards at various locations (within the Asama Shrine precincts). }} '''Asama Shrine''' (Sengen Jinja) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Shibamoto-chō, {{ill|lt=Mishima City|Q653478|WD=Q653478}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}. It was formerly the Sannomiya, later the Ninomiya, of {{ill|lt=Izumi Province|Q860928|WD=Q860928}}. It is one of the {{ill|lt=Asama Shrine|Q3471785|WD=Q3471785}} shrines. === Overview === In ancient times it was one of the eight auxiliary shrines (Hassho Bekū) of {{ill|lt=Mishima Taisha|Q650772|WD=Q650772}}{{Sfn|History of Mishima City, Vol. 2|1988|p=558}}. It was also regarded as a {{ill|lt=Sessha|Q135009904|WD=Q135009904}} (subsidiary shrine), but is now independent. Although it is listed as a candidate shrine for the Shikinaisha “Iga Muhime no Mikoto Shrine,” it is not considered a leading theory. Generally, this Himusubi no Mikoto Shrine is thought to be the Raiden Shrine, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|lt=Izusan Shrine|Q611460|WD=Q611460}} in {{ill|lt=Atami City|Q653364|WD=Q653364}}, and there is a theory that this shrine served as its distant worship site{{Sfn|Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System|2000|p=162}}. The shrine name is said to have shifted from the Buddhist name “Sengen” of the honji-butsu (original Buddhist manifestation) of Izusan Shrine—{{ill|lt=Senju Kannon|Q10904910|WD=Q10904910|lt=Eleven-Faced Thousand-Armed Thousand-Eyed Kannon}}—to the kami name “Asama”{{Sfn|Revised Tōshū Shikō, Vol. 8 (Upper)|p=35}}. According to the “{{ill|lt=Izumi Province Shrine Rank Register|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087}},” its divine rank is “Senior First Rank, Thousand-Eyed Great Bodhisattva.” It was originally the Sannomiya of Izumi Province, but after the Ninomiya Hachimangū (present-day Wakamiya Shrine) was transferred to the grounds of the grand shrine, it became the Ninomiya{{Sfn|Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System|2000|p=162}}. However, there is no record that the Shinnomiya, Hirose Shrine, was elevated to Sannomiya{{Sfn|Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System|2000|p=162}}, and one theory holds that the Sannomiya was Yōhara Shrine{{Sfn|History of Mishima City, Vol. 2|1988|p=559}}. The annual festival is held on July 15 and 16. It is said that in the past, people always visited this shrine when making a {{ill|lt=Mount Fuji pilgrimage|Q11456115|WD=Q11456115}}. In addition, the spring water around the precincts served as irrigation water for downstream towns and villages. === Enshrined deities === [[File:Sengen-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|thumb|Asama Shrine worship hall]] * Principal deities ** {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto}} ** Hafuhime no Mikoto * Associated deities ** {{ill|lt=Ninigi|Q1056017|WD=Q1056017|lt=Ninigi no Mikoto}} ** {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|lt=Hoakari no Mikoto}} ** {{ill|lt=Hohoderi|Q10731395|WD=Q10731395|lt=Hoderi no Mikoto}} ** {{ill|lt=Hoori|Q1051960|WD=Q1051960|lt=Hoori no Mikoto}} Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto is the daughter of the Mishima Ōkami ({{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi no Mikoto}}), the enshrined deity of Mishima Taisha. According to the “{{ill|lt=Miyake-ki|Q17215100|WD=Q17215100}},” Hafuhime no Mikoto was a consort whom the Mishima Ōkami placed on {{ill|lt=Izu Ōshima|Q1070580|WD=Q1070580|lt=Ōshima}}. Furthermore, the associated deity Ninigi no Mikoto is the husband of Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto, and Hoakari no Mikoto, Hoderi no Mikoto, and Hoori no Mikoto are their sons. In other words, the shrine enshrines the wife and daughter of the deity of Mishima Taisha, as well as the daughter’s family. === Shrine within the precincts === [[File:Shibaoka Shrine.jpg|thumb|Shibaoka Shrine]] ; Shibaoka Shrine :* Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Takami-Musubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi no Kami}} :* Shrine rank: {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system#Minor shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Village shrine}} :* Annual festival: July 15–16 : It is enshrined to the left of the main hall of Asama Shrine. Originally, it was a residence shrine enshrined on the estate of the Seko Rokudayū family of the Seko honjin, but in {{ill|lt=Meiji 4|Q11512280|WD=Q11512280}} ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746}} or {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}}) it came to be worshiped as the tutelary shrine of Shiba Town and was ranked as a village shrine. In March {{ill|lt=1877|Q7773|WD=Q7773}} (Meiji 10), it was destroyed in the great fire of {{ill|lt=Mishima-juku|Q524171|WD=Q524171}}, but was restored in {{ill|lt=1880|Q7806|WD=Q7806}} (Meiji 13). In July 1952 it was merged into Asama Shrine and relocated to the present site together with its shrine building. Beneath the main hall is a large circular stone called the “spirit stone,” which is regarded as a guardian deity for children. === Sources === [[File:Sengen Shrine (Mishima).jpg|thumb|Asama Shrine torii]] === References and websites === * Explanatory boards within the precincts '''Historical materials''' * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=Compiled by Akira Akiyama; revised by Shōhei Hagiwara|author=|year=|title=Revised Tōshū Shikō, Vol. 8 (Upper)|publisher=|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Revised Tōshū Shikō, Vol. 8 (Upper)}}}} ** {{PDFlink|[https://archive.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kosho/ru04/ru04_00291/ru04_00291_0010/ru04_00291_0010.pdf “Revised Tōshū Shikō, Vol. 8 (Upper)”]}} (Waseda University Library), consulted. '''Books''' * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=History of Mishima City Editorial Committee|author=|year=1988|title=History of Mishima City, Vol. 2|publisher=Mishima City|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|History of Mishima City, Vol. 2|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System Research Association|author=|year=2000|chapter=|title=Foundational Research on the Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System|publisher=Iwata Shoin|isbn=978-4872941708|ref={{Harvid|Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System|2000}}}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Asama Shrine|Q3471785|WD=Q3471785}} * {{ill|lt=Mishima Taisha|Q650772|WD=Q650772}} * {{ill|lt=Izusan Shrine|Q611460|WD=Q611460}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Izumi Province|Q11380552|WD=Q11380552}} * {{ill|lt=Izumi Province Shrine Rank Register|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087}} === External links === * [http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402034 Asama Shrine] – Shizuoka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines {{DEFAULTSORT:sengenjinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sengen Shrine (Izu Province Ni-no-Miya)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sengen Shrine (Izu Province Ni-no-Miya)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Ni-no-Miya]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Successor]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:三島市の歴史]] [[Category:三島市の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社|小せんけんしんしや]] [[Category:二宮]] [[Category:三宮 (社格)]] [[Category:伊豆国|社2]] [[Category:浅間神社|せんけんしんしや みしまし]]

(Mishima)}} [[JA:浅間神社 (三島市)]] {{wikidata link|Q30925511}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name=Asama Shrine |Image=[[File:Sengen Shrine (Mishima) and Shibaoka Shrine.jpg|250px]]
Shibaoka Shrine (left) and Asama Shrine (right)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|7|23|N|138|54|49|E}}}} |Location=6-3 Shibamoto-chō, Mishima City, Shizuoka Prefecture | Latitude degrees = 35 | Latitude minutes = 07 | Latitude seconds = 23.0 | N (North latitude) or S (South latitude) = N | Longitude degrees = 138 | Longitude minutes = 54 | Longitude seconds = 49.0 | E (East longitude) or W (West longitude) = E | Map country code = JP-22 | Map = Japan Shizuoka | Label position=bottom |Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}
Hafuhime no Mikoto |Founded= |Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} debated shrine (minor)
Later {{ill|lt=Izumi Province|Q860928|WD=Q860928|1=Izu Province}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Sannomiya|1=Ichinomiya}}, then {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya#Ninomiya, Sannomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Ninomiya|1=Ichinomiya}}{{Sfn|Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System|2000|p=162}} |Main hall= |Annual festival={{ill|lt=July 15|Q2686|WD=Q2686|1=July 15}}・{{ill|lt=July 16|Q2731|WD=Q2731|lt=16th|1=July 16}}Explanatory boards at various locations (within the Asama Shrine precincts). }} '''Asama Shrine''' (Sengen Jinja) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Shibamoto-chō, {{ill|lt=Mishima City|Q653478|WD=Q653478|1=Mishima, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. It was formerly the Sannomiya, later the Ninomiya, of {{ill|lt=Izumi Province|Q860928|WD=Q860928|1=Izu Province}}. It is one of the {{ill|lt=Asama Shrine|Q3471785|WD=Q3471785|1=Sengen shrine}} shrines. === Overview === In ancient times it was one of the eight auxiliary shrines (Hassho Bekū) of {{ill|lt=Mishima Taisha|Q650772|WD=Q650772|1=Mishima Taisha}}{{Sfn|History of Mishima City, Vol. 2|1988|p=558}}. It was also regarded as a {{ill|lt=Sessha|Q135009904|WD=Q135009904|1=Sessha}} (subsidiary shrine), but is now independent. Although it is listed as a candidate shrine for the Shikinaisha “Iga Muhime no Mikoto Shrine,” it is not considered a leading theory. Generally, this Himusubi no Mikoto Shrine is thought to be the Raiden Shrine, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|lt=Izusan Shrine|Q611460|WD=Q611460|1=Izusan Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Atami City|Q653364|WD=Q653364|1=Atami}}, and there is a theory that this shrine served as its distant worship site{{Sfn|Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System|2000|p=162}}. The shrine name is said to have shifted from the Buddhist name “Sengen” of the honji-butsu (original Buddhist manifestation) of Izusan Shrine—{{ill|lt=Senju Kannon|Q10904910|WD=Q10904910|lt=Eleven-Faced Thousand-Armed Thousand-Eyed Kannon|1=Thousand-armed Kannon}}—to the kami name “Asama”{{Sfn|Revised Tōshū Shikō, Vol. 8 (Upper)|p=35}}. According to the “{{ill|lt=Izumi Province Shrine Rank Register|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087|1=Izumi Province Shrine Rank Register}},” its divine rank is “Senior First Rank, Thousand-Eyed Great Bodhisattva.” It was originally the Sannomiya of Izumi Province, but after the Ninomiya Hachimangū (present-day Wakamiya Shrine) was transferred to the grounds of the grand shrine, it became the Ninomiya{{Sfn|Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System|2000|p=162}}. However, there is no record that the Shinnomiya, Hirose Shrine, was elevated to Sannomiya{{Sfn|Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System|2000|p=162}}, and one theory holds that the Sannomiya was Yōhara Shrine{{Sfn|History of Mishima City, Vol. 2|1988|p=559}}. The annual festival is held on July 15 and 16. It is said that in the past, people always visited this shrine when making a {{ill|lt=Mount Fuji pilgrimage|Q11456115|WD=Q11456115|1=mountaineering in Mount Fuji}}. In addition, the spring water around the precincts served as irrigation water for downstream towns and villages. === Enshrined deities === [[File:Sengen-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|thumb|Asama Shrine worship hall]] * Principal deities ** {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} ** Hafuhime no Mikoto * Associated deities ** {{ill|lt=Ninigi|Q1056017|WD=Q1056017|lt=Ninigi no Mikoto|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}} ** {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|lt=Hoakari no Mikoto|1=Amenohoakari}} ** {{ill|lt=Hohoderi|Q10731395|WD=Q10731395|lt=Hoderi no Mikoto|1=Hosuseri}} ** {{ill|lt=Hoori|Q1051960|WD=Q1051960|lt=Hoori no Mikoto|1=Hoori}} Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto is the daughter of the Mishima Ōkami ({{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi no Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}}), the enshrined deity of Mishima Taisha. According to the “{{ill|lt=Miyake-ki|Q17215100|WD=Q17215100|1=Miyake-ki}},” Hafuhime no Mikoto was a consort whom the Mishima Ōkami placed on {{ill|lt=Izu Ōshima|Q1070580|WD=Q1070580|lt=Ōshima|1=Izu Ōshima}}. Furthermore, the associated deity Ninigi no Mikoto is the husband of Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto, and Hoakari no Mikoto, Hoderi no Mikoto, and Hoori no Mikoto are their sons. In other words, the shrine enshrines the wife and daughter of the deity of Mishima Taisha, as well as the daughter’s family. === Shrine within the precincts === [[File:Shibaoka Shrine.jpg|thumb|Shibaoka Shrine]] ; Shibaoka Shrine :* Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Takami-Musubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi no Kami|1=Takamimusubi}} :* Shrine rank: {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system#Minor shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} :* Annual festival: July 15–16 : It is enshrined to the left of the main hall of Asama Shrine. Originally, it was a residence shrine enshrined on the estate of the Seko Rokudayū family of the Seko honjin, but in {{ill|lt=Meiji 4|Q11512280|WD=Q11512280|1=Meiji – year 4}} ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746|1=1871}} or {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}) it came to be worshiped as the tutelary shrine of Shiba Town and was ranked as a village shrine. In March {{ill|lt=1877|Q7773|WD=Q7773|1=1877}} (Meiji 10), it was destroyed in the great fire of {{ill|lt=Mishima-juku|Q524171|WD=Q524171|1=Mishima-shuku}}, but was restored in {{ill|lt=1880|Q7806|WD=Q7806|1=1880}} (Meiji 13). In July 1952 it was merged into Asama Shrine and relocated to the present site together with its shrine building. Beneath the main hall is a large circular stone called the “spirit stone,” which is regarded as a guardian deity for children. === Sources === [[File:Sengen Shrine (Mishima).jpg|thumb|Asama Shrine torii]] === References and websites === * Explanatory boards within the precincts '''Historical materials''' * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=Compiled by Akira Akiyama; revised by Shōhei Hagiwara|author=|year=|title=Revised Tōshū Shikō, Vol. 8 (Upper)|publisher=|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Revised Tōshū Shikō, Vol. 8 (Upper)}}}} ** {{PDFlink|[https://archive.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kosho/ru04/ru04_00291/ru04_00291_0010/ru04_00291_0010.pdf “Revised Tōshū Shikō, Vol. 8 (Upper)”]}} (Waseda University Library), consulted. '''Books''' * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=History of Mishima City Editorial Committee|author=|year=1988|title=History of Mishima City, Vol. 2|publisher=Mishima City|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|History of Mishima City, Vol. 2|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System Research Association|author=|year=2000|chapter=|title=Foundational Research on the Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System|publisher=Iwata Shoin|isbn=978-4872941708|ref={{Harvid|Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System|2000}}}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Asama Shrine|Q3471785|WD=Q3471785|1=Sengen shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Mishima Taisha|Q650772|WD=Q650772|1=Mishima Taisha}} * {{ill|lt=Izusan Shrine|Q611460|WD=Q611460|1=Izusan Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Izumi Province|Q11380552|WD=Q11380552|1=List of Shikinaisha in Izu Province}} * {{ill|lt=Izumi Province Shrine Rank Register|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087|1=Izumi Province Shrine Rank Register}} === External links === * [http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402034 Asama Shrine] – Shizuoka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines {{DEFAULTSORT:sengenjinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sengen Shrine (Izu Province Ni-no-Miya)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sengen Shrine (Izu Province Ni-no-Miya)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Ni-no-Miya]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Successor]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:三島市の歴史]] [[Category:三島市の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社|小せんけんしんしや]] [[Category:二宮]] [[Category:三宮 (社格)]] [[Category:伊豆国|社2]] [[Category:浅間神社|せんけんしんしや みしまし]]

(Mishima)}} [[JA:浅間神社 (三島市)]] {{wikidata link|Q30925511}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name=Asama Shrine |Image=[[File:Sengen Shrine (Mishima) and Shibaoka Shrine.jpg|250px]]
Shibaoka Shrine (left) and Asama Shrine (right)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|7|23|N|138|54|49|E}}}} |Location=6-3 Shibamoto-chō, Mishima City, Shizuoka Prefecture | Latitude degrees = 35 | Latitude minutes = 07 | Latitude seconds = 23.0 | N (North latitude) or S (South latitude) = N | Longitude degrees = 138 | Longitude minutes = 54 | Longitude seconds = 49.0 | E (East longitude) or W (West longitude) = E | Map country code = JP-22 | Map = Japan Shizuoka | Label position=bottom |Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}
Hafuhime no Mikoto |Founded= |Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} debated shrine (minor)
Later {{ill|lt=Izumi Province|Q860928|WD=Q860928|1=Izu Province}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Sannomiya|1=Ichinomiya}}, then {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya#Ninomiya, Sannomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Ninomiya|1=Ichinomiya}}{{Sfn|Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System|2000|p=162}} |Main hall= |Annual festival={{ill|lt=July 15|Q2686|WD=Q2686|1=July 15}}・{{ill|lt=July 16|Q2731|WD=Q2731|lt=16th|1=July 16}}Explanatory boards at various locations (within the Asama Shrine precincts). }} '''Asama Shrine''' (Sengen Jinja) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Shibamoto-chō, {{ill|lt=Mishima City|Q653478|WD=Q653478|1=Mishima, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. It was formerly the Sannomiya, later the Ninomiya, of {{ill|lt=Izumi Province|Q860928|WD=Q860928|1=Izu Province}}. It is one of the {{ill|lt=Asama Shrine|Q3471785|WD=Q3471785|1=Sengen shrine}} shrines. === Overview === In ancient times it was one of the eight auxiliary shrines (Hassho Bekū) of {{ill|lt=Mishima Taisha|Q650772|WD=Q650772|1=Mishima Taisha}}{{Sfn|History of Mishima City, Vol. 2|1988|p=558}}. It was also regarded as a {{ill|lt=Sessha|Q135009904|WD=Q135009904|1=Sessha}} (subsidiary shrine), but is now independent. Although it is listed as a candidate shrine for the Shikinaisha “Iga Muhime no Mikoto Shrine,” it is not considered a leading theory. Generally, this Himusubi no Mikoto Shrine is thought to be the Raiden Shrine, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|lt=Izusan Shrine|Q611460|WD=Q611460|1=Izusan Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Atami City|Q653364|WD=Q653364|1=Atami}}, and there is a theory that this shrine served as its distant worship site{{Sfn|Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System|2000|p=162}}. The shrine name is said to have shifted from the Buddhist name “Sengen” of the honji-butsu (original Buddhist manifestation) of Izusan Shrine—{{ill|lt=Senju Kannon|Q10904910|WD=Q10904910|lt=Eleven-Faced Thousand-Armed Thousand-Eyed Kannon|1=Thousand-armed Kannon}}—to the kami name “Asama”{{Sfn|Revised Tōshū Shikō, Vol. 8 (Upper)|p=35}}. According to the “{{ill|lt=Izumi Province Shrine Rank Register|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087|1=Izumi Province Shrine Rank Register}},” its divine rank is “Senior First Rank, Thousand-Eyed Great Bodhisattva.” It was originally the Sannomiya of Izumi Province, but after the Ninomiya Hachimangū (present-day Wakamiya Shrine) was transferred to the grounds of the grand shrine, it became the Ninomiya{{Sfn|Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System|2000|p=162}}. However, there is no record that the Shinnomiya, Hirose Shrine, was elevated to Sannomiya{{Sfn|Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System|2000|p=162}}, and one theory holds that the Sannomiya was Yōhara Shrine{{Sfn|History of Mishima City, Vol. 2|1988|p=559}}. The annual festival is held on July 15 and 16. It is said that in the past, people always visited this shrine when making a {{ill|lt=Mount Fuji pilgrimage|Q11456115|WD=Q11456115|1=mountaineering in Mount Fuji}}. In addition, the spring water around the precincts served as irrigation water for downstream towns and villages. === Enshrined deities === [[File:Sengen-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|thumb|Asama Shrine worship hall]] * Principal deities ** {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} ** Hafuhime no Mikoto * Associated deities ** {{ill|lt=Ninigi|Q1056017|WD=Q1056017|lt=Ninigi no Mikoto|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}} ** {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|lt=Hoakari no Mikoto|1=Amenohoakari}} ** {{ill|lt=Hohoderi|Q10731395|WD=Q10731395|lt=Hoderi no Mikoto|1=Hosuseri}} ** {{ill|lt=Hoori|Q1051960|WD=Q1051960|lt=Hoori no Mikoto|1=Hoori}} Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto is the daughter of the Mishima Ōkami ({{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi no Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}}), the enshrined deity of Mishima Taisha. According to the “{{ill|lt=Miyake-ki|Q17215100|WD=Q17215100|1=Miyake-ki}},” Hafuhime no Mikoto was a consort whom the Mishima Ōkami placed on {{ill|lt=Izu Ōshima|Q1070580|WD=Q1070580|lt=Ōshima|1=Izu Ōshima}}. Furthermore, the associated deity Ninigi no Mikoto is the husband of Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto, and Hoakari no Mikoto, Hoderi no Mikoto, and Hoori no Mikoto are their sons. In other words, the shrine enshrines the wife and daughter of the deity of Mishima Taisha, as well as the daughter’s family. === Shrine within the precincts === [[File:Shibaoka Shrine.jpg|thumb|Shibaoka Shrine]] ; Shibaoka Shrine :* Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Takami-Musubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi no Kami|1=Takamimusubi}} :* Shrine rank: {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system#Minor shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} :* Annual festival: July 15–16 : It is enshrined to the left of the main hall of Asama Shrine. Originally, it was a residence shrine enshrined on the estate of the Seko Rokudayū family of the Seko honjin, but in {{ill|lt=Meiji 4|Q11512280|WD=Q11512280|1=Meiji – year 4}} ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746|1=1871}} or {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}) it came to be worshiped as the tutelary shrine of Shiba Town and was ranked as a village shrine. In March {{ill|lt=1877|Q7773|WD=Q7773|1=1877}} (Meiji 10), it was destroyed in the great fire of {{ill|lt=Mishima-juku|Q524171|WD=Q524171|1=Mishima-shuku}}, but was restored in {{ill|lt=1880|Q7806|WD=Q7806|1=1880}} (Meiji 13). In July 1952 it was merged into Asama Shrine and relocated to the present site together with its shrine building. Beneath the main hall is a large circular stone called the “spirit stone,” which is regarded as a guardian deity for children. === Sources === [[File:Sengen Shrine (Mishima).jpg|thumb|Asama Shrine torii]] === References and websites === * Explanatory boards within the precincts '''Historical materials''' * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=Compiled by Akira Akiyama; revised by Shōhei Hagiwara|author=|year=|title=Revised Tōshū Shikō, Vol. 8 (Upper)|publisher=|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Revised Tōshū Shikō, Vol. 8 (Upper)}}}} ** {{PDFlink|[https://archive.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kosho/ru04/ru04_00291/ru04_00291_0010/ru04_00291_0010.pdf “Revised Tōshū Shikō, Vol. 8 (Upper)”]}} (Waseda University Library), consulted. '''Books''' * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=History of Mishima City Editorial Committee|author=|year=1988|title=History of Mishima City, Vol. 2|publisher=Mishima City|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|History of Mishima City, Vol. 2|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System Research Association|author=|year=2000|chapter=|title=Foundational Research on the Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System|publisher=Iwata Shoin|isbn=978-4872941708|ref={{Harvid|Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System|2000}}}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Asama Shrine|Q3471785|WD=Q3471785|1=Sengen shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Mishima Taisha|Q650772|WD=Q650772|1=Mishima Taisha}} * {{ill|lt=Izusan Shrine|Q611460|WD=Q611460|1=Izusan Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Izumi Province|Q11380552|WD=Q11380552|1=List of Shikinaisha in Izu Province}} * {{ill|lt=Izumi Province Shrine Rank Register|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087|1=Izumi Province Shrine Rank Register}} === External links === * [http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402034 Asama Shrine] – Shizuoka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines {{DEFAULTSORT:sengenjinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sengen Shrine (Izu Province Ni-no-Miya)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sengen Shrine (Izu Province Ni-no-Miya)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Ni-no-Miya]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Successor]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:三島市の歴史]] [[Category:三島市の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社|小せんけんしんしや]] [[Category:二宮]] [[Category:三宮 (社格)]] [[Category:伊豆国|社2]] [[Category:浅間神社|せんけんしんしや みしまし]]

(Mishima)}} [[JA:浅間神社 (三島市)]] {{wikidata link|Q30925511}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name=Asama Shrine |Image=[[File:Sengen Shrine (Mishima) and Shibaoka Shrine.jpg|250px]]
Shibaoka Shrine (left) and Asama Shrine (right)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|7|23|N|138|54|49|E}}}} |Location=6-3 Shibamoto-chō, Mishima City, Shizuoka Prefecture | Latitude degrees = 35 | Latitude minutes = 07 | Latitude seconds = 23.0 | N (North latitude) or S (South latitude) = N | Longitude degrees = 138 | Longitude minutes = 54 | Longitude seconds = 49.0 | E (East longitude) or W (West longitude) = E | Map country code = JP-22 | Map = Japan Shizuoka | Label position=bottom |Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}
Hafuhime no Mikoto |Founded= |Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} debated shrine (minor)
Later {{ill|lt=Izumi Province|Q860928|WD=Q860928|1=Izu Province}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Sannomiya|1=Ichinomiya}}, then {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya#Ninomiya, Sannomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Ninomiya|1=Ichinomiya}}{{Sfn|Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System|2000|p=162}} |Main hall= |Annual festival={{ill|lt=July 15|Q2686|WD=Q2686|1=July 15}}・{{ill|lt=July 16|Q2731|WD=Q2731|lt=16th|1=July 16}}Explanatory boards at various locations (within the Asama Shrine precincts). }} '''Asama Shrine''' (Sengen Jinja) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Shibamoto-chō, {{ill|lt=Mishima City|Q653478|WD=Q653478|1=Mishima, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. It was formerly the Sannomiya, later the Ninomiya, of {{ill|lt=Izumi Province|Q860928|WD=Q860928|1=Izu Province}}. It is one of the {{ill|lt=Asama Shrine|Q3471785|WD=Q3471785|1=Sengen shrine}} shrines. === Overview === In ancient times it was one of the eight auxiliary shrines (Hassho Bekū) of {{ill|lt=Mishima Taisha|Q650772|WD=Q650772|1=Mishima Taisha}}{{Sfn|History of Mishima City, Vol. 2|1988|p=558}}. It was also regarded as a {{ill|lt=Sessha|Q135009904|WD=Q135009904|1=Sessha}} (subsidiary shrine), but is now independent. Although it is listed as a candidate shrine for the Shikinaisha “Iga Muhime no Mikoto Shrine,” it is not considered a leading theory. Generally, this Himusubi no Mikoto Shrine is thought to be the Raiden Shrine, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|lt=Izusan Shrine|Q611460|WD=Q611460|1=Izusan Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Atami City|Q653364|WD=Q653364|1=Atami}}, and there is a theory that this shrine served as its distant worship site{{Sfn|Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System|2000|p=162}}. The shrine name is said to have shifted from the Buddhist name “Sengen” of the honji-butsu (original Buddhist manifestation) of Izusan Shrine—{{ill|lt=Senju Kannon|Q10904910|WD=Q10904910|lt=Eleven-Faced Thousand-Armed Thousand-Eyed Kannon|1=Thousand-armed Kannon}}—to the kami name “Asama”{{Sfn|Revised Tōshū Shikō, Vol. 8 (Upper)|p=35}}. According to the “{{ill|lt=Izumi Province Shrine Rank Register|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087|1=Izumi Province Shrine Rank Register}},” its divine rank is “Senior First Rank, Thousand-Eyed Great Bodhisattva.” It was originally the Sannomiya of Izumi Province, but after the Ninomiya Hachimangū (present-day Wakamiya Shrine) was transferred to the grounds of the grand shrine, it became the Ninomiya{{Sfn|Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System|2000|p=162}}. However, there is no record that the Shinnomiya, Hirose Shrine, was elevated to Sannomiya{{Sfn|Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System|2000|p=162}}, and one theory holds that the Sannomiya was Yōhara Shrine{{Sfn|History of Mishima City, Vol. 2|1988|p=559}}. The annual festival is held on July 15 and 16. It is said that in the past, people always visited this shrine when making a {{ill|lt=Mount Fuji pilgrimage|Q11456115|WD=Q11456115|1=mountaineering in Mount Fuji}}. In addition, the spring water around the precincts served as irrigation water for downstream towns and villages. === Enshrined deities === [[File:Sengen-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|thumb|Asama Shrine worship hall]] * Principal deities ** {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} ** Hafuhime no Mikoto * Associated deities ** {{ill|lt=Ninigi|Q1056017|WD=Q1056017|lt=Ninigi no Mikoto|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}} ** {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|lt=Hoakari no Mikoto|1=Amenohoakari}} ** {{ill|lt=Hohoderi|Q10731395|WD=Q10731395|lt=Hoderi no Mikoto|1=Hosuseri}} ** {{ill|lt=Hoori|Q1051960|WD=Q1051960|lt=Hoori no Mikoto|1=Hoori}} Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto is the daughter of the Mishima Ōkami ({{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi no Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}}), the enshrined deity of Mishima Taisha. According to the “{{ill|lt=Miyake-ki|Q17215100|WD=Q17215100|1=Miyake-ki}},” Hafuhime no Mikoto was a consort whom the Mishima Ōkami placed on {{ill|lt=Izu Ōshima|Q1070580|WD=Q1070580|lt=Ōshima|1=Izu Ōshima}}. Furthermore, the associated deity Ninigi no Mikoto is the husband of Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto, and Hoakari no Mikoto, Hoderi no Mikoto, and Hoori no Mikoto are their sons. In other words, the shrine enshrines the wife and daughter of the deity of Mishima Taisha, as well as the daughter’s family. === Shrine within the precincts === [[File:Shibaoka Shrine.jpg|thumb|Shibaoka Shrine]] ; Shibaoka Shrine :* Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Takami-Musubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi no Kami|1=Takamimusubi}} :* Shrine rank: {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system#Minor shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} :* Annual festival: July 15–16 : It is enshrined to the left of the main hall of Asama Shrine. Originally, it was a residence shrine enshrined on the estate of the Seko Rokudayū family of the Seko honjin, but in {{ill|lt=Meiji 4|Q11512280|WD=Q11512280|1=Meiji – year 4}} ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746|1=1871}} or {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}) it came to be worshiped as the tutelary shrine of Shiba Town and was ranked as a village shrine. In March {{ill|lt=1877|Q7773|WD=Q7773|1=1877}} (Meiji 10), it was destroyed in the great fire of {{ill|lt=Mishima-juku|Q524171|WD=Q524171|1=Mishima-shuku}}, but was restored in {{ill|lt=1880|Q7806|WD=Q7806|1=1880}} (Meiji 13). In July 1952 it was merged into Asama Shrine and relocated to the present site together with its shrine building. Beneath the main hall is a large circular stone called the “spirit stone,” which is regarded as a guardian deity for children. === Sources === [[File:Sengen Shrine (Mishima).jpg|thumb|Asama Shrine torii]] === References and websites === * Explanatory boards within the precincts '''Historical materials''' * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=Compiled by Akira Akiyama; revised by Shōhei Hagiwara|author=|year=|title=Revised Tōshū Shikō, Vol. 8 (Upper)|publisher=|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Revised Tōshū Shikō, Vol. 8 (Upper)}}}} ** {{PDFlink|[https://archive.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kosho/ru04/ru04_00291/ru04_00291_0010/ru04_00291_0010.pdf “Revised Tōshū Shikō, Vol. 8 (Upper)”]}} (Waseda University Library), consulted. '''Books''' * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=History of Mishima City Editorial Committee|author=|year=1988|title=History of Mishima City, Vol. 2|publisher=Mishima City|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|History of Mishima City, Vol. 2|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System Research Association|author=|year=2000|chapter=|title=Foundational Research on the Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System|publisher=Iwata Shoin|isbn=978-4872941708|ref={{Harvid|Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System|2000}}}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Asama Shrine|Q3471785|WD=Q3471785|1=Sengen shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Mishima Taisha|Q650772|WD=Q650772|1=Mishima Taisha}} * {{ill|lt=Izusan Shrine|Q611460|WD=Q611460|1=Izusan Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Izumi Province|Q11380552|WD=Q11380552|1=List of Shikinaisha in Izu Province}} * {{ill|lt=Izumi Province Shrine Rank Register|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087|1=Izumi Province Shrine Rank Register}} === External links === * [http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402034 Asama Shrine] – Shizuoka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sengen Shrine (Izu Province Ni-no-Miya)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sengen Shrine (Izu Province Ni-no-Miya)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Ni-no-Miya]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Successor]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:三島市の歴史]] [[Category:三島市の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社|小せんけんしんしや]] [[Category:二宮]] [[Category:三宮 (社格)]] [[Category:伊豆国|社2]] [[Category:浅間神社|せんけんしんしや みしまし]]

(Mishima)}} [[JA:浅間神社 (三島市)]] {{wikidata link|Q30925511}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name=Asama Shrine |Image=[[File:Sengen Shrine (Mishima) and Shibaoka Shrine.jpg|250px]]
Shibaoka Shrine (left) and Asama Shrine (right)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|7|23|N|138|54|49|E}}}} |Location=6-3 Shibamoto-chō, Mishima City, Shizuoka Prefecture | Latitude degrees = 35 | Latitude minutes = 07 | Latitude seconds = 23.0 | N (North latitude) or S (South latitude) = N | Longitude degrees = 138 | Longitude minutes = 54 | Longitude seconds = 49.0 | E (East longitude) or W (West longitude) = E | Map country code = JP-22 | Map = Japan Shizuoka | Label position=bottom |Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}
Hafuhime no Mikoto |Founded= |Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} debated shrine (minor)
Later {{ill|lt=Izumi Province|Q860928|WD=Q860928|1=Izu Province}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Sannomiya|1=Ichinomiya}}, then {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya#Ninomiya, Sannomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Ninomiya|1=Ichinomiya}}{{Sfn|Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System|2000|p=162}} |Main hall= |Annual festival={{ill|lt=July 15|Q2686|WD=Q2686|1=July 15}}・{{ill|lt=July 16|Q2731|WD=Q2731|lt=16th|1=July 16}}Explanatory boards at various locations (within the Asama Shrine precincts). }} '''Asama Shrine''' (Sengen Jinja) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Shibamoto-chō, {{ill|lt=Mishima City|Q653478|WD=Q653478|1=Mishima, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. It was formerly the Sannomiya, later the Ninomiya, of {{ill|lt=Izumi Province|Q860928|WD=Q860928|1=Izu Province}}. It is one of the {{ill|lt=Asama Shrine|Q3471785|WD=Q3471785|1=Sengen shrine}} shrines. === Overview === In ancient times it was one of the eight auxiliary shrines (Hassho Bekū) of {{ill|lt=Mishima Taisha|Q650772|WD=Q650772|1=Mishima Taisha}}{{Sfn|History of Mishima City, Vol. 2|1988|p=558}}. It was also regarded as a {{ill|lt=Sessha|Q135009904|WD=Q135009904|1=Sessha}} (subsidiary shrine), but is now independent. Although it is listed as a candidate shrine for the Shikinaisha “Iga Muhime no Mikoto Shrine,” it is not considered a leading theory. Generally, this Himusubi no Mikoto Shrine is thought to be the Raiden Shrine, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|lt=Izusan Shrine|Q611460|WD=Q611460|1=Izusan Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Atami City|Q653364|WD=Q653364|1=Atami}}, and there is a theory that this shrine served as its distant worship site{{Sfn|Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System|2000|p=162}}. The shrine name is said to have shifted from the Buddhist name “Sengen” of the honji-butsu (original Buddhist manifestation) of Izusan Shrine—{{ill|lt=Senju Kannon|Q10904910|WD=Q10904910|lt=Eleven-Faced Thousand-Armed Thousand-Eyed Kannon|1=Thousand-armed Kannon}}—to the kami name “Asama”{{Sfn|Revised Tōshū Shikō, Vol. 8 (Upper)|p=35}}. According to the “{{ill|lt=Izumi Province Shrine Rank Register|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087|1=Izumi Province Shrine Rank Register}},” its divine rank is “Senior First Rank, Thousand-Eyed Great Bodhisattva.” It was originally the Sannomiya of Izumi Province, but after the Ninomiya Hachimangū (present-day Wakamiya Shrine) was transferred to the grounds of the grand shrine, it became the Ninomiya{{Sfn|Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System|2000|p=162}}. However, there is no record that the Shinnomiya, Hirose Shrine, was elevated to Sannomiya{{Sfn|Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System|2000|p=162}}, and one theory holds that the Sannomiya was Yōhara Shrine{{Sfn|History of Mishima City, Vol. 2|1988|p=559}}. The annual festival is held on July 15 and 16. It is said that in the past, people always visited this shrine when making a {{ill|lt=Mount Fuji pilgrimage|Q11456115|WD=Q11456115|1=mountaineering in Mount Fuji}}. In addition, the spring water around the precincts served as irrigation water for downstream towns and villages. === Enshrined deities === [[File:Sengen-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|thumb|Asama Shrine worship hall]] * Principal deities ** {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} ** Hafuhime no Mikoto * Associated deities ** {{ill|lt=Ninigi|Q1056017|WD=Q1056017|lt=Ninigi no Mikoto|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}} ** {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|lt=Hoakari no Mikoto|1=Amenohoakari}} ** {{ill|lt=Hohoderi|Q10731395|WD=Q10731395|lt=Hoderi no Mikoto|1=Hosuseri}} ** {{ill|lt=Hoori|Q1051960|WD=Q1051960|lt=Hoori no Mikoto|1=Hoori}} Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto is the daughter of the Mishima Ōkami ({{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi no Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}}), the enshrined deity of Mishima Taisha. According to the “{{ill|lt=Miyake-ki|Q17215100|WD=Q17215100|1=Miyake-ki}},” Hafuhime no Mikoto was a consort whom the Mishima Ōkami placed on {{ill|lt=Izu Ōshima|Q1070580|WD=Q1070580|lt=Ōshima|1=Izu Ōshima}}. Furthermore, the associated deity Ninigi no Mikoto is the husband of Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto, and Hoakari no Mikoto, Hoderi no Mikoto, and Hoori no Mikoto are their sons. In other words, the shrine enshrines the wife and daughter of the deity of Mishima Taisha, as well as the daughter’s family. === Shrine within the precincts === [[File:Shibaoka Shrine.jpg|thumb|Shibaoka Shrine]] ; Shibaoka Shrine :* Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Takami-Musubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi no Kami|1=Takamimusubi}} :* Shrine rank: {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system#Minor shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} :* Annual festival: July 15–16 : It is enshrined to the left of the main hall of Asama Shrine. Originally, it was a residence shrine enshrined on the estate of the Seko Rokudayū family of the Seko honjin, but in {{ill|lt=Meiji 4|Q11512280|WD=Q11512280|1=Meiji – year 4}} ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746|1=1871}} or {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}) it came to be worshiped as the tutelary shrine of Shiba Town and was ranked as a village shrine. In March {{ill|lt=1877|Q7773|WD=Q7773|1=1877}} (Meiji 10), it was destroyed in the great fire of {{ill|lt=Mishima-juku|Q524171|WD=Q524171|1=Mishima-shuku}}, but was restored in {{ill|lt=1880|Q7806|WD=Q7806|1=1880}} (Meiji 13). In July 1952 it was merged into Asama Shrine and relocated to the present site together with its shrine building. Beneath the main hall is a large circular stone called the “spirit stone,” which is regarded as a guardian deity for children. === Sources === [[File:Sengen Shrine (Mishima).jpg|thumb|Asama Shrine torii]] === References and websites === * Explanatory boards within the precincts '''Historical materials''' * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=Compiled by Akira Akiyama; revised by Shōhei Hagiwara|author=|year=|title=Revised Tōshū Shikō, Vol. 8 (Upper)|publisher=|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Revised Tōshū Shikō, Vol. 8 (Upper)}}}} ** {{PDFlink|[https://archive.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kosho/ru04/ru04_00291/ru04_00291_0010/ru04_00291_0010.pdf “Revised Tōshū Shikō, Vol. 8 (Upper)”]}} (Waseda University Library), consulted. '''Books''' * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=History of Mishima City Editorial Committee|author=|year=1988|title=History of Mishima City, Vol. 2|publisher=Mishima City|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|History of Mishima City, Vol. 2|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System Research Association|author=|year=2000|chapter=|title=Foundational Research on the Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System|publisher=Iwata Shoin|isbn=978-4872941708|ref={{Harvid|Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System|2000}}}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Asama Shrine|Q3471785|WD=Q3471785|1=Sengen shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Mishima Taisha|Q650772|WD=Q650772|1=Mishima Taisha}} * {{ill|lt=Izusan Shrine|Q611460|WD=Q611460|1=Izusan Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Izumi Province|Q11380552|WD=Q11380552|1=List of Shikinaisha in Izu Province}} * {{ill|lt=Izumi Province Shrine Rank Register|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087|1=Izumi Province Shrine Rank Register}} === External links === * [http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402034 Asama Shrine] – Shizuoka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sengen Shrine (Izu Province Ni-no-Miya)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sengen Shrine (Izu Province Ni-no-Miya)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Ni-no-Miya]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Successor]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:三島市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings in Mishima, Shizuoka]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社|小せんけんしんしや]] [[Category:二宮]] [[Category:三宮 (社格)]] [[Category:伊豆国|社2]] [[Category:浅間神社|せんけんしんしや みしまし]]

(Mishima)}} [[JA:浅間神社 (三島市)]] {{wikidata link|Q30925511}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name=Asama Shrine |Image=[[File:Sengen Shrine (Mishima) and Shibaoka Shrine.jpg|250px]]
Shibaoka Shrine (left) and Asama Shrine (right)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|7|23|N|138|54|49|E}}}} |Location=6-3 Shibamoto-chō, Mishima City, Shizuoka Prefecture | Latitude degrees = 35 | Latitude minutes = 07 | Latitude seconds = 23.0 | N (North latitude) or S (South latitude) = N | Longitude degrees = 138 | Longitude minutes = 54 | Longitude seconds = 49.0 | E (East longitude) or W (West longitude) = E | Map country code = JP-22 | Map = Japan Shizuoka | Label position=bottom |Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}
Hafuhime no Mikoto |Founded= |Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} debated shrine (minor)
Later {{ill|lt=Izumi Province|Q860928|WD=Q860928|1=Izu Province}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Sannomiya|1=Ichinomiya}}, then {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya#Ninomiya, Sannomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Ninomiya|1=Ichinomiya}}{{Sfn|Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System|2000|p=162}} |Main hall= |Annual festival={{ill|lt=July 15|Q2686|WD=Q2686|1=July 15}}・{{ill|lt=July 16|Q2731|WD=Q2731|lt=16th|1=July 16}}Explanatory boards at various locations (within the Asama Shrine precincts). }} '''Asama Shrine''' (Sengen Jinja) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Shibamoto-chō, {{ill|lt=Mishima City|Q653478|WD=Q653478|1=Mishima, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. It was formerly the Sannomiya, later the Ninomiya, of {{ill|lt=Izumi Province|Q860928|WD=Q860928|1=Izu Province}}. It is one of the {{ill|lt=Asama Shrine|Q3471785|WD=Q3471785|1=Sengen shrine}} shrines. === Overview === In ancient times it was one of the eight auxiliary shrines (Hassho Bekū) of {{ill|lt=Mishima Taisha|Q650772|WD=Q650772|1=Mishima Taisha}}{{Sfn|History of Mishima City, Vol. 2|1988|p=558}}. It was also regarded as a {{ill|lt=Sessha|Q135009904|WD=Q135009904|1=Sessha}} (subsidiary shrine), but is now independent. Although it is listed as a candidate shrine for the Shikinaisha “Iga Muhime no Mikoto Shrine,” it is not considered a leading theory. Generally, this Himusubi no Mikoto Shrine is thought to be the Raiden Shrine, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|lt=Izusan Shrine|Q611460|WD=Q611460|1=Izusan Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Atami City|Q653364|WD=Q653364|1=Atami}}, and there is a theory that this shrine served as its distant worship site{{Sfn|Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System|2000|p=162}}. The shrine name is said to have shifted from the Buddhist name “Sengen” of the honji-butsu (original Buddhist manifestation) of Izusan Shrine—{{ill|lt=Senju Kannon|Q10904910|WD=Q10904910|lt=Eleven-Faced Thousand-Armed Thousand-Eyed Kannon|1=Thousand-armed Kannon}}—to the kami name “Asama”{{Sfn|Revised Tōshū Shikō, Vol. 8 (Upper)|p=35}}. According to the “{{ill|lt=Izumi Province Shrine Rank Register|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087|1=Izumi Province Shrine Rank Register}},” its divine rank is “Senior First Rank, Thousand-Eyed Great Bodhisattva.” It was originally the Sannomiya of Izumi Province, but after the Ninomiya Hachimangū (present-day Wakamiya Shrine) was transferred to the grounds of the grand shrine, it became the Ninomiya{{Sfn|Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System|2000|p=162}}. However, there is no record that the Shinnomiya, Hirose Shrine, was elevated to Sannomiya{{Sfn|Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System|2000|p=162}}, and one theory holds that the Sannomiya was Yōhara Shrine{{Sfn|History of Mishima City, Vol. 2|1988|p=559}}. The annual festival is held on July 15 and 16. It is said that in the past, people always visited this shrine when making a {{ill|lt=Mount Fuji pilgrimage|Q11456115|WD=Q11456115|1=mountaineering in Mount Fuji}}. In addition, the spring water around the precincts served as irrigation water for downstream towns and villages. === Enshrined deities === [[File:Sengen-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|thumb|Asama Shrine worship hall]] * Principal deities ** {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} ** Hafuhime no Mikoto * Associated deities ** {{ill|lt=Ninigi|Q1056017|WD=Q1056017|lt=Ninigi no Mikoto|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}} ** {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|lt=Hoakari no Mikoto|1=Amenohoakari}} ** {{ill|lt=Hohoderi|Q10731395|WD=Q10731395|lt=Hoderi no Mikoto|1=Hosuseri}} ** {{ill|lt=Hoori|Q1051960|WD=Q1051960|lt=Hoori no Mikoto|1=Hoori}} Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto is the daughter of the Mishima Ōkami ({{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi no Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}}), the enshrined deity of Mishima Taisha. According to the “{{ill|lt=Miyake-ki|Q17215100|WD=Q17215100|1=Miyake-ki}},” Hafuhime no Mikoto was a consort whom the Mishima Ōkami placed on {{ill|lt=Izu Ōshima|Q1070580|WD=Q1070580|lt=Ōshima|1=Izu Ōshima}}. Furthermore, the associated deity Ninigi no Mikoto is the husband of Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto, and Hoakari no Mikoto, Hoderi no Mikoto, and Hoori no Mikoto are their sons. In other words, the shrine enshrines the wife and daughter of the deity of Mishima Taisha, as well as the daughter’s family. === Shrine within the precincts === [[File:Shibaoka Shrine.jpg|thumb|Shibaoka Shrine]] ; Shibaoka Shrine :* Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Takami-Musubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi no Kami|1=Takamimusubi}} :* Shrine rank: {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system#Minor shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} :* Annual festival: July 15–16 : It is enshrined to the left of the main hall of Asama Shrine. Originally, it was a residence shrine enshrined on the estate of the Seko Rokudayū family of the Seko honjin, but in {{ill|lt=Meiji 4|Q11512280|WD=Q11512280|1=Meiji – year 4}} ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746|1=1871}} or {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}) it came to be worshiped as the tutelary shrine of Shiba Town and was ranked as a village shrine. In March {{ill|lt=1877|Q7773|WD=Q7773|1=1877}} (Meiji 10), it was destroyed in the great fire of {{ill|lt=Mishima-juku|Q524171|WD=Q524171|1=Mishima-shuku}}, but was restored in {{ill|lt=1880|Q7806|WD=Q7806|1=1880}} (Meiji 13). In July 1952 it was merged into Asama Shrine and relocated to the present site together with its shrine building. Beneath the main hall is a large circular stone called the “spirit stone,” which is regarded as a guardian deity for children. === Sources === [[File:Sengen Shrine (Mishima).jpg|thumb|Asama Shrine torii]] === References and websites === * Explanatory boards within the precincts '''Historical materials''' * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=Compiled by Akira Akiyama; revised by Shōhei Hagiwara|author=|year=|title=Revised Tōshū Shikō, Vol. 8 (Upper)|publisher=|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Revised Tōshū Shikō, Vol. 8 (Upper)}}}} ** {{PDFlink|[https://archive.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kosho/ru04/ru04_00291/ru04_00291_0010/ru04_00291_0010.pdf “Revised Tōshū Shikō, Vol. 8 (Upper)”]}} (Waseda University Library), consulted. '''Books''' * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=History of Mishima City Editorial Committee|author=|year=1988|title=History of Mishima City, Vol. 2|publisher=Mishima City|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|History of Mishima City, Vol. 2|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System Research Association|author=|year=2000|chapter=|title=Foundational Research on the Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System|publisher=Iwata Shoin|isbn=978-4872941708|ref={{Harvid|Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System|2000}}}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Asama Shrine|Q3471785|WD=Q3471785|1=Sengen shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Mishima Taisha|Q650772|WD=Q650772|1=Mishima Taisha}} * {{ill|lt=Izusan Shrine|Q611460|WD=Q611460|1=Izusan Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Izumi Province|Q11380552|WD=Q11380552|1=List of Shikinaisha in Izu Province}} * {{ill|lt=Izumi Province Shrine Rank Register|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087|1=Izumi Province Shrine Rank Register}} === External links === * [http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402034 Asama Shrine] – Shizuoka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sengen Shrine (Izu Province Ni-no-Miya)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sengen Shrine (Izu Province Ni-no-Miya)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Ni-no-Miya]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Successor]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:三島市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings in Mishima, Shizuoka]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社|小せんけんしんしや]] [[Category:二宮]] [[Category:Shrine rank of Sannomiya]] [[Category:伊豆国|社2]] [[Category:浅間神社|せんけんしんしや みしまし]]

(Mishima)}} [[JA:浅間神社 (三島市)]] {{wikidata link|Q30925511}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name=Asama Shrine |Image=[[File:Sengen Shrine (Mishima) and Shibaoka Shrine.jpg|250px]]
Shibaoka Shrine (left) and Asama Shrine (right)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|7|23|N|138|54|49|E}}}} |Location=6-3 Shibamoto-chō, Mishima City, Shizuoka Prefecture | Latitude degrees = 35 | Latitude minutes = 07 | Latitude seconds = 23.0 | N (North latitude) or S (South latitude) = N | Longitude degrees = 138 | Longitude minutes = 54 | Longitude seconds = 49.0 | E (East longitude) or W (West longitude) = E | Map country code = JP-22 | Map = Japan Shizuoka | Label position=bottom |Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}
Hafuhime no Mikoto |Founded= |Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} debated shrine (minor)
Later {{ill|lt=Izumi Province|Q860928|WD=Q860928|1=Izu Province}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Sannomiya|1=Ichinomiya}}, then {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya#Ninomiya, Sannomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Ninomiya|1=Ichinomiya}}{{Sfn|Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System|2000|p=162}} |Main hall= |Annual festival={{ill|lt=July 15|Q2686|WD=Q2686|1=July 15}}・{{ill|lt=July 16|Q2731|WD=Q2731|lt=16th|1=July 16}}Explanatory boards at various locations (within the Asama Shrine precincts). }} '''Asama Shrine''' (Sengen Jinja) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Shibamoto-chō, {{ill|lt=Mishima City|Q653478|WD=Q653478|1=Mishima, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. It was formerly the Sannomiya, later the Ninomiya, of {{ill|lt=Izumi Province|Q860928|WD=Q860928|1=Izu Province}}. It is one of the {{ill|lt=Asama Shrine|Q3471785|WD=Q3471785|1=Sengen shrine}} shrines. === Overview === In ancient times it was one of the eight auxiliary shrines (Hassho Bekū) of {{ill|lt=Mishima Taisha|Q650772|WD=Q650772|1=Mishima Taisha}}{{Sfn|History of Mishima City, Vol. 2|1988|p=558}}. It was also regarded as a {{ill|lt=Sessha|Q135009904|WD=Q135009904|1=Sessha}} (subsidiary shrine), but is now independent. Although it is listed as a candidate shrine for the Shikinaisha “Iga Muhime no Mikoto Shrine,” it is not considered a leading theory. Generally, this Himusubi no Mikoto Shrine is thought to be the Raiden Shrine, a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|lt=Izusan Shrine|Q611460|WD=Q611460|1=Izusan Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Atami City|Q653364|WD=Q653364|1=Atami}}, and there is a theory that this shrine served as its distant worship site{{Sfn|Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System|2000|p=162}}. The shrine name is said to have shifted from the Buddhist name “Sengen” of the honji-butsu (original Buddhist manifestation) of Izusan Shrine—{{ill|lt=Senju Kannon|Q10904910|WD=Q10904910|lt=Eleven-Faced Thousand-Armed Thousand-Eyed Kannon|1=Thousand-armed Kannon}}—to the kami name “Asama”{{Sfn|Revised Tōshū Shikō, Vol. 8 (Upper)|p=35}}. According to the “{{ill|lt=Izumi Province Shrine Rank Register|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087|1=Izumi Province Shrine Rank Register}},” its divine rank is “Senior First Rank, Thousand-Eyed Great Bodhisattva.” It was originally the Sannomiya of Izumi Province, but after the Ninomiya Hachimangū (present-day Wakamiya Shrine) was transferred to the grounds of the grand shrine, it became the Ninomiya{{Sfn|Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System|2000|p=162}}. However, there is no record that the Shinnomiya, Hirose Shrine, was elevated to Sannomiya{{Sfn|Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System|2000|p=162}}, and one theory holds that the Sannomiya was Yōhara Shrine{{Sfn|History of Mishima City, Vol. 2|1988|p=559}}. The annual festival is held on July 15 and 16. It is said that in the past, people always visited this shrine when making a {{ill|lt=Mount Fuji pilgrimage|Q11456115|WD=Q11456115|1=mountaineering in Mount Fuji}}. In addition, the spring water around the precincts served as irrigation water for downstream towns and villages. === Enshrined deities === [[File:Sengen-jinja (Mishima, Shizuoka).JPG|thumb|Asama Shrine worship hall]] * Principal deities ** {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} ** Hafuhime no Mikoto * Associated deities ** {{ill|lt=Ninigi|Q1056017|WD=Q1056017|lt=Ninigi no Mikoto|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}} ** {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|lt=Hoakari no Mikoto|1=Amenohoakari}} ** {{ill|lt=Hohoderi|Q10731395|WD=Q10731395|lt=Hoderi no Mikoto|1=Hosuseri}} ** {{ill|lt=Hoori|Q1051960|WD=Q1051960|lt=Hoori no Mikoto|1=Hoori}} Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto is the daughter of the Mishima Ōkami ({{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi no Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}}), the enshrined deity of Mishima Taisha. According to the “{{ill|lt=Miyake-ki|Q17215100|WD=Q17215100|1=Miyake-ki}},” Hafuhime no Mikoto was a consort whom the Mishima Ōkami placed on {{ill|lt=Izu Ōshima|Q1070580|WD=Q1070580|lt=Ōshima|1=Izu Ōshima}}. Furthermore, the associated deity Ninigi no Mikoto is the husband of Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto, and Hoakari no Mikoto, Hoderi no Mikoto, and Hoori no Mikoto are their sons. In other words, the shrine enshrines the wife and daughter of the deity of Mishima Taisha, as well as the daughter’s family. === Shrine within the precincts === [[File:Shibaoka Shrine.jpg|thumb|Shibaoka Shrine]] ; Shibaoka Shrine :* Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Takami-Musubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi no Kami|1=Takamimusubi}} :* Shrine rank: {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system#Minor shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} :* Annual festival: July 15–16 : It is enshrined to the left of the main hall of Asama Shrine. Originally, it was a residence shrine enshrined on the estate of the Seko Rokudayū family of the Seko honjin, but in {{ill|lt=Meiji 4|Q11512280|WD=Q11512280|1=Meiji – year 4}} ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746|1=1871}} or {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}) it came to be worshiped as the tutelary shrine of Shiba Town and was ranked as a village shrine. In March {{ill|lt=1877|Q7773|WD=Q7773|1=1877}} (Meiji 10), it was destroyed in the great fire of {{ill|lt=Mishima-juku|Q524171|WD=Q524171|1=Mishima-shuku}}, but was restored in {{ill|lt=1880|Q7806|WD=Q7806|1=1880}} (Meiji 13). In July 1952 it was merged into Asama Shrine and relocated to the present site together with its shrine building. Beneath the main hall is a large circular stone called the “spirit stone,” which is regarded as a guardian deity for children. === Sources === [[File:Sengen Shrine (Mishima).jpg|thumb|Asama Shrine torii]] === References and websites === * Explanatory boards within the precincts '''Historical materials''' * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=Compiled by Akira Akiyama; revised by Shōhei Hagiwara|author=|year=|title=Revised Tōshū Shikō, Vol. 8 (Upper)|publisher=|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Revised Tōshū Shikō, Vol. 8 (Upper)}}}} ** {{PDFlink|[https://archive.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kosho/ru04/ru04_00291/ru04_00291_0010/ru04_00291_0010.pdf “Revised Tōshū Shikō, Vol. 8 (Upper)”]}} (Waseda University Library), consulted. '''Books''' * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=History of Mishima City Editorial Committee|author=|year=1988|title=History of Mishima City, Vol. 2|publisher=Mishima City|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|History of Mishima City, Vol. 2|1988}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System Research Association|author=|year=2000|chapter=|title=Foundational Research on the Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System|publisher=Iwata Shoin|isbn=978-4872941708|ref={{Harvid|Medieval Provincial Ichinomiya System|2000}}}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Asama Shrine|Q3471785|WD=Q3471785|1=Sengen shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Mishima Taisha|Q650772|WD=Q650772|1=Mishima Taisha}} * {{ill|lt=Izusan Shrine|Q611460|WD=Q611460|1=Izusan Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Izumi Province|Q11380552|WD=Q11380552|1=List of Shikinaisha in Izu Province}} * {{ill|lt=Izumi Province Shrine Rank Register|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087|1=Izumi Province Shrine Rank Register}} === External links === * [http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4402034 Asama Shrine] – Shizuoka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sengen Shrine (Izu Province Ni-no-Miya)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sengen Shrine (Izu Province Ni-no-Miya)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Ni-no-Miya]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Successor]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:三島市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings in Mishima, Shizuoka]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社|小せんけんしんしや]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:Shrine rank of Sannomiya]] [[Category:伊豆国|社2]] [[Category:浅間神社|せんけんしんしや みしまし]]
